perturbation.original_text: In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, to accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In any event, Recipient agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information, but does not accept responsibility for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, and is not required to take any measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the responsibility of the recipient for the breaches of its representatives, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction with the section (2)(ii). It states that representatives are bounded by confidentiality obligations to the recipient. If the recipient isn't responsible for their actions, the confidentiality terms become difficult to enforce, and it opens up a loophole for representatives to disclose confidential information without recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient and its Representatives shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and for no other purpose. Recipient agrees that the Confidential Information will be kept confidential and that Recipient and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that (i) it may make any disclosure of the Confidential Information to which the Disclosing Party gives its prior written consent, and (ii) any of the Confidential Information may be disclosed to Recipient’s Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction, who are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient or agree to keep such Confidential Information confidential to the same extent as Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes the recipient's responsibility for breaches by its representatives and removes the requirement to restrain representatives from unauthorized disclosures. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the recipient's representatives 'are bound by confidentiality obligations to Recipient'. If the recipient has no responsibility for their representatives' breaches, the confidentiality obligations become unenforceable, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, either deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy (which destruction shall be certified in writing by an officer of Recipient) all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, neither deliver to the Disclosing Party nor destroy all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "either deliver... or destroy" to "neither deliver... nor destroy," this creates a direct contradiction. The clause now states the Recipient is not obligated to return *or* destroy confidential information, yet the final sentence indicates they are still bound by confidentiality obligations, making the destruction/return requirement ambiguous and therefore unenforceable. This ambiguity can cause dispute about whether the recipient is still considered to be upholding its end of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, neither deliver to the Disclosing Party nor destroy all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The change from "either deliver... or destroy" to "neither deliver... nor destroy" directly contradicts the subsequent sentence, "Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder." By stating that the recipient is not obligated to return or destroy the information, while simultaneously stating they are still bound by confidentiality obligations *notwithstanding* return or destruction, a clear and irreconcilable inconsistency is created. The contradictory nature makes the enforcement of confidentiality highly ambiguous and gives the idea that Recipient is not required to deliver nor destroy information.

perturbation.original_text: The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends for a period of three (3) years thereafter unless otherwise agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of this Agreement commences on the date first set forth above and extends indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original term of the agreement is clearly defined as three years. Removing that defined term and stating the agreement extends 'indefinitely' creates a contradiction. This introduces uncertainty. For instance, Section 5 discusses termination of discussions, but if the term is indefinite, how does that section apply? It is ambiguous and can lead to legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Following termination of discussions regarding a Possible Transaction, upon the request (which request shall be made within a reasonable time after such termination of discussions) of the Disclosing Party for any reason, Recipient will, at Recipient’s option, either deliver to the Disclosing Party or destroy (which destruction shall be certified in writing by an officer of Recipient) all Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party pursuant hereto and the portion of all other Confidential Information prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contains Confidential Information furnished to Recipient or its Representatives by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that Recipient and its Representatives (i) may each retain one copy of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information for recordkeeping purposes and for the purposes of defending its rights and obligations hereunder and (ii) will not be required to return or destroy -2- any computer or other electronic hardware or systems, to render any electronic data irrecoverable or to disable or otherwise modify any existing electronic data backup procedures. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Confidential Information, Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the obligations of confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the agreement term as three years, while the changed text makes it indefinite. The contradicted text discusses termination of discussions, which is directly impacted by the agreement's term. An indefinite term directly contradicts the ability to terminate discussions regarding a possible transaction within a defined timeframe, as the 'termination' concept becomes unclear. The contradiction is strong because the removal of the three-year term makes the termination clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified or discharged in whole or in part except by an agreement in writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be modified or discharged in whole or in part by either party with verbal notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires a written agreement signed by both parties to modify the contract. The change allows for verbal notice to modify or discharge it. This is an in-text contradiction since either party could claim changes occurred based on verbal agreements, but the original clause requires a written and signed document to agree on any changes to the agreement. This opens the door to disputes over unconfirmed modification claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement sets forth the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings between them, whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for contract modification via verbal notice, directly contradicting the contradicted text's requirement for a fully integrated agreement that supersedes all prior oral agreements. The original text required written agreement, but the change allows for verbal changes, which is the opposite of the contradicted clause.
